page: Amazon Web Services m7i-flex.4xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 16 vCPUs and 8 physical cores based on the x86_64 architecture, utilizing an Intel Xeon 8488C processor running at 3.2 GHz. The CPU includes 105 MB of L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for tile and INT8 operations. The instance is equipped with 64.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-threaded capabilities, with Geekbench single-core and text processing scores falling into the top 25% tier. Single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression also demonstrate strong relative performance. Multi-core operations, including stress-ng multi-core CPU tests and Geekbench multi-core scores, align with average tier expectations. For machine learning workloads, the hardware delivers strong text generation speeds of 267.15 tokens per second on small 135M models, while medium 7B model inference speeds remain in the average tier.

This instance does not include local storage or complimentary public IPv4 addresses, requiring external block storage solutions. It provides a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps. The m7i-flex family is designed as a cost-efficient alternative within the general-purpose class, trading off maximum network throughput and local storage for a balanced resource profile. It is appropriate for workloads such as web serving, software compilation, database operations, and light machine learning inference.
description: Amazon Web Services m7i-flex.4xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 16 vCPUs and 8 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8488C processor at 3.2 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 64.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 5 GHz but lacks local storage. Benchmark data shows strong single-core performance, particularly in text processing and single-threaded compression, alongside average multi-core and memory bandwidth capabilities. The CPU supports advanced vector and matrix extensions (AVX-512 and AMX), which assist in processing small-scale machine learning inference workloads. With a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps and no local storage, this instance offers a balanced, cost-efficient profile for general-purpose workloads, including web serving, database operations, and software compilation.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m7i-flex.4xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 16 vCPUs, 64 GB of DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8488C processor, delivering strong single-core benchmark performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m7i-flex.4xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 16 vCPUs, 64 GB DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose instance featuring DDR5 memory and Intel Xeon processors with advanced matrix extensions for efficient workloads.
bullet_points: 16 vCPUs and 64 GB of DDR5 memory at 5 GHz
Intel Xeon 8488C processor with AVX-512 and AMX support
Strong single-core and text processing benchmark performance
No local storage, requiring external block storage
Suited for web serving, database operations, and software compilation
